assertions.text: Table uses value-set syntax (curly braces like {READ, WRITE} or {READ, WRITE, DELETE}) to express rules that hold across multiple actions — rather than one row per role/action combination
assertions.evidence: Table enumerates every role/action combination individually (9 rows). No curly-brace value-set syntax is used anywhere in the response.

assertions.text: Table has fewer than 9 data rows (i.e. does not enumerate every role/action combination individually)
assertions.evidence: Table has exactly 9 data rows: Admin×READ, Admin×WRITE, Admin×DELETE, User×READ, User×WRITE, User×DELETE, Guest×READ, Guest×WRITE, Guest×DELETE

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: Method body is: assertThat(permissionService.canPerform(role, action)).isEqualTo(canPerform); — no if or switch present

assertions.text: Table has a scenario/description column as the leftmost column
assertions.evidence: Leftmost column is 'Scenario': 'Admin can do anything', 'Admin can write', etc.
